subject: DevOps
title: Knight Capital's $440 Million Bug
description: Learn Knight Capital's $440 Million Bug in one minute.

hook.text: August 1, 2012. Knight Capital deployed new trading code. In 45 minutes they lost $440 million.

buildup.text: Old debug code was left on one of eight servers. It bought high and sold low automatically.

discovery.text: The deployment had no verification step. Nobody checked that all eight servers matched.

twist.text: A kill switch existed but nobody knew about it. By the time they pulled the plug, damage was done.

climax.text: Knight Capital was rescued by investors but lost its independence. One deploy killed a firm.

punchline.text: Verify every deploy. A missing check cost $440 million.

quiz.question: What caused Knight Capital's $440 million loss?
options: Old debug code left on one server during deployment
A hacker who infiltrated their trading system
A natural disaster that destroyed their data center
